corrections.text: The article presents inside quotation marks the phrase "lost its Senate-confirmed head Gail Slater in February" attributed to The Capitol Forum. The Capitol Forum's actual wording is "lost its Senate-confirmed antitrust head, Gail Slater, when the White House ousted her in February." The quoted text compresses the source (dropping "antitrust" and the clause "when the White House ousted her") rather than reproducing it verbatim. The substance — that the division lost its Senate-confirmed head Gail Slater in February — is accurate and supported by the source.
